heading: Dumping permit program
label: 1412 Dumping permit program
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (c)(4), means , which is classified generally to this chapter, chapter 41 (§ 2801 et seq.) of this title, and chapters 32 (§ 1431 et seq.) and 32A (§ 1447 et seq.) of Title 16, Conservation.Pub. L. 92–532

children.label: A
children.text: The need for the proposed dumping.

children.label: B
children.text: The effect of such dumping on human health and welfare, including economic, esthetic, and recreational values.

children.label: C
children.text: The effect of such dumping on fisheries resources, plankton, fish, shellfish, wildlife, shore lines and beaches.

children.label: i
children.text: the transfer, concentration, and dispersion of such material and its byproducts through biological, physical, and chemical processes.

children.label: ii
children.text: potential changes in marine ecosystem diversity, productivity, and stability, and

children.label: iii
children.text: species and community population dynamics.

children.label: E
children.text: The persistence and permanence of the effects of the dumping.

children.label: F
children.text: The effect of dumping particular volumes and concentrations of such materials.

children.label: G
children.text: Appropriate locations and methods of disposal or recycling, including land-based alternatives and the probable impact of requiring use of such alternate locations or methods upon considerations affecting the public interest.

children.label: H
children.text: The effect on alternate uses of oceans, such as scientific study, fishing, and other living resource exploitation, and non-living resource exploitation.

children.label: I
children.text: In designating recommended sites, the Administrator shall utilize wherever feasible locations beyond the edge of the Continental Shelf.

children.heading: Permit categories
children.label: b Permit categories
children.text: The Administrator may establish and issue various categories of permits, including the general permits described in .section 1414(c) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall, in a manner consistent with the criteria established pursuant to subsection (a), designate sites or time periods for dumping. The Administrator shall designate sites or time periods for dumping that will mitigate adverse impact on the environment to the greatest extent practicable.

children.heading: Prohibitions regarding site or time period
children.label: 2 Prohibitions regarding site or time period
children.text: In any case where the Administrator determines that, with respect to certain materials, it is necessary to prohibit dumping at a site or during a time period, the Administrator shall prohibit the dumping of such materials in such site or during such time period. This prohibition shall apply to any dumping at the site or during such time period. This prohibition shall apply to any dumping at the site or during the time period, including any dumping under .section 1413(e) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a baseline assessment of conditions at the site;

children.label: B
children.text: a program for monitoring the site;

children.label: C
children.text: special management conditions or practices to be implemented at each site that are necessary for protection of the environment;

children.label: D
children.text: consideration of the quantity of the material to be disposed of at the site, and the presence, nature, and bioavailability of the contaminants in the material;

children.label: E
children.text: consideration of the anticipated use of the site over the long term, including the anticipated closure date for the site, if applicable, and any need for management of the site after the closure of the site; and

children.label: F
children.text: a schedule for review and revision of the plan (which shall not be reviewed and revised less frequently than 10 years after adoption of the plan, and every 10 years thereafter).

children.heading: General site management plan requirement; prohibitions
children.label: 4 General site management plan requirement; prohibitions
children.text: After , no site shall receive a final designation unless a management plan has been developed pursuant to this section. Beginning on , no permit for dumping pursuant to this Act or authorization for dumping under shall be issued for a site (other than the site located off the coast of Newport Beach, California, which is known as “LA–3”) unless such site has received a final designation pursuant to this subsection or an alternative site has been selected pursuant to . Beginning , no permit for dumping pursuant to this Act or authorization for dumping under shall be issued for the site located off the coast of Newport Beach, California, which is known as “LA–3”, unless such site has received a final designation pursuant to this subsection or an alternative site has been selected pursuant to .January 1, 1995January 1, 1997January 1, 2011section 1413(e) of this titlesection 1413(b) of this titlesection 1413(e) of this titlesection 1413(b) of this title

children.heading: Management plans for previously designated sites
children.label: 5 Management plans for previously designated sites
children.text: The Administrator shall develop a site management plan for any site designated prior to , as expeditiously as practicable, but not later than , giving priority consideration to management plans for designated sites that are considered to have the greatest impact on the environment.January 1, 1995January 1, 1997

children.heading: Fish wastes
children.label: d Fish wastes
children.text: No permit is required under this subchapter for the transportation for dumping or the dumping of fish wastes, except when deposited in harbors or other protected or enclosed coastal waters, or where the Administrator finds that such deposits could endanger health, the environment, or ecological systems in a specific location. Where the Administrator makes such a finding, such material may be deposited only as authorized by a permit issued by the Administrator under this section.

children.heading: Foreign State permits; acceptance
children.label: e Foreign State permits; acceptance
children.text: In the case of transportation of material, by an agency or instrumentality of the United States or by a vessel or aircraft registered in the United States or flying the United States flag, from a location in a foreign State Party to the Convention, a permit issued pursuant to the authority of that foreign State Party, in accordance with Convention requirements, and which otherwise could have been issued pursuant to subsection (a) of this section, shall be accepted, for the purposes of this subchapter, as if it were issued by the Administrator under the authority of this section: , That in the case of an agency or instrumentality of the United States, no application shall be made for a permit to be issued pursuant to the authority of a foreign State Party to the Convention unless the Administrator concurs in the filing of such application.Provided
